request.messages: [redacted: candidate evidence omitted from raw judge artifact]

message.reasoning_content:
We are asked to validate candidates for ASHRAE Guideline 36 knowledge items. The candidates are provided in a JSON array. We need to output a JSON object with a "verdicts" array containing one verdict per candidate, with fields: candidate_id, is_valid_guideline36_knowledge (boolean), reason (short string), category_if_not (null or one of: front_matter, toc, generic_definition, unsupported, duplicate, other).

The instructions: "Accept an item only if it is useful, grounded ASHRAE Guideline 36 operational knowledge. Reject legal/front matter, TOC text, generic definitions without operational use, duplicated/noisy extraction, or summaries not supported by evidence."

So we need to examine each candidate and decide if it is valid.

Candidates:

1. cand_5a7161645d6c3b81: Title: "Exhaust Fan Start/Stop - Zone Group Occupancy", Section: 5.19.1.1. Summary: "Exhaust fan shall operate when any of the associated system supply fans is proven on and any associated Zone Group is in the Occupied Mode." Evidence quote matches. This seems like an operational sequence from the guideline. It is grounded, useful operational knowledge. Should be accepted.

2. cand_e4447700f88d4f5f: Title: "Exhaust Fan Start/Stop - Room Temperature Cycling", Section: 5.19.1.1. Summary: "Exhaust fan shall run when zone temperature rises above the active cooling setpoint and stop when zone temperature falls more than 1°C (2°F) below the active cooling setpoint for 2 minutes." Evidence quote: "Exhaust fan shall run when zone temperature rises above the active cooling setpoint until zone temperature falls more than 1°C (2°F) below the active cooling setpoint for 2 minutes." This is also operational knowledge. It appears to be a specific sequence. However, note that both candidates 1 and 2 have the same section 5.19.1.1. That section might contain multiple start/stop methods. So they are not duplicates but different methods. Valid.

3. cand_29a3a4013c7121a4: Title: "Room Temperature Control Method Restriction", Section: 5.19.1.1. Summary: "The room temperature control method should only be used in non-occupied spaces where ventilation is not required (e.g., equipment rooms)." Evidence quote matches. This is application guidance. It restricts the use of the room temperature control method. It is grounded operational guidance. Should be accepted.

4. cand_899d803a86d9d854: Title: "Fan Maintenance Interval Alarm", Section: 5.19.2.1. Summary: "Generate a Level 4 alarm when fan run hours exceed 3,000. Reset the interval counter when the alarm is acknowledged." Evidence: "Maintenance interval alarm when fan has operated for more than 3,000 hours: Level 4. Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged." This is a fault diagnostic rule. It is operational knowledge. Accept.

5. cand_1d54a816f297dafc: Title: "Fan Status Mismatch Alarm", Section: 5.19.2.2. Summary: "Alarm if fan status differs from command for 15 seconds. Commanded on/status off is Level 2; commanded off/status off is Level 4." Evidence quote matches. This is also a fault diagnostic rule. Operational knowledge. Accept.

All candidates seem valid. They are all from the guideline, have specific operational content, are not generic definitions, not front matter, etc. They are grounded with evidence quotes. So all should be accepted. We'll output "is_valid_guideline36_knowledge": true for each. The reason can be short, e.g., "Grounded operational knowledge from Guideline 36." category_if_not will be null for all since they are valid.

JSON response:
{
  "verdicts": [
    {
      "candidate_id": "cand_5a7161645d6c3b81",
      "is_valid_guideline36_knowledge": true,
      "reason": "Grounded operational sequence from Guideline 36.",
      "category_if_not": null
    },
    {
      "candidate_id": "cand_e4447700f88d4f5f",
      "is_valid_guideline36_knowledge": true,
      "reason": "Grounded operational sequence from Guideline 36.",
      "category_if_not": null
    },
    {
      "candidate_id": "cand_29a3a4013c7121a4",
      "is_valid_guideline36_knowledge": true,
      "reason": "Grounded application guidance from Guideline 36.",
      "category_if_not": null
    },
    {
      "candidate_id": "cand_899d803a86d9d854",
      "is_valid_guideline36_knowledge": true,
      "reason": "Grounded fault diagnostic rule from Guideline 36.",
      "category_if_not": null
    },
    {
      "candidate_id": "cand_1d54a816f297dafc",
      "is_valid_guideline36_knowledge": true,
      "reason": "Grounded fault diagnostic rule from Guideline 36.",
      "category_if_not": null
    }
  ]
}
